channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: HOLY. FUCK. — 32 replies — 128.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HOLY. FUCK.

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-02 17:11 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62301820
@grok is this legal?

62301873
>>62301796
iirc I don't think the wash sale rule applies to crypto yet, so yes you can if that is true. You might get in trouble if your dealing with a significant sum though or like sell enough to deduct your entire taxable income and your job isn't a Mcwage job, the IRS has a catch-22 called the economic substance doctrine where they could argue that you were trying to fleece them in an audit or court proceeding.

62301881
There are actively managed funds that intentionally do stuff like this to get you tax breaks every year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62301884
>>62301873
Capital losses can only be used to offset capital gains, not wage income.

62301886
>>62301796
the IRS doesn't mind if you do this

62301897
>>62301884
Yeah you're right

62301905
>>62301820
No, it isn't. It's a wash sale unless at least a month has passed.
Besides, there are state that cap your losses for deduction to something like 3k per year. You can't report more than that.

62301920
>>62301796
Also OP You can only offset your capital gains to 3k in that case, but you can rollover beyond that I think indefinitely. Don't take this drunkies advice to heart though it's just what I remember from filling out my own tax forms

62301931
>>62301905
could probably buy back into a bitcoin etf and get away with it...wont be able to buy illicit goods anymore though

62301947
>>62301920
I thought it was unlimited against capital gains but limited against income.

62302187
>>62301947
Prettu sure it isn't. 3k a year, tops. So good luck with that 13 years writing off your shitcoin loss :)

62302188
>>62302187
not true it offsets capitalized gains

62302190
>>62302187
Nah I'm right. Capital losses have unlimited offset against capital gains as long as they are both short or long term. The $3k limit your talking about is offsetting regular income with capital losses

62302206
>>62301931
just buy WBTC lmao

62302609
>>62301884
You can deduct up to 3000 dollars a year in capital losses from your earned income

62302619
>>62301796
And then when you sell it at 100k at a real loss of 26k you have to pay taxes on fake capital gains of 11k.
Fucking idiot

62302625
Creative account hack

62302635
Illegal in my country.

62302646
>>62301905
in germany it's legal.

62302875
Yes and I do this but I'm in the UK so you have to wait 30 days to buy back (bed & breakfast rule), or you can swap it to another token like WBTC.

62303255
>>62301796
False. the us gov only accepts 3k in losses per year.

62303270
>>62301884
Wrong, I've taken the max deduction every single year for the past 5 years because I blow at trading

62303329
>>62301796
You can do this assuming the investment isn’t subject to wash sale rules. You can also swap between bitcoin ETFs that aren’t considered substantially identical. However doing this is going to make your cost basis much lower so when you sell in the future at a profit, you’ll have higher levels of taxable gains.

62303351
>>62303255
You can harvest unlimited losses to offset capital gains. If you sell stock A at a 5 mil profit and sell stock B at a 3 mil loss, you’ll only have 2 mil in taxable gains that year. The 3k limit only applies to lowering MAGI.

62303503
>people on /biz/ dont understand how capital gains taxes work
jesus christ guys get it together

62303568
>>62303270
based

62303876
>>62301905
There is no wash sales on crypto because its not a security, its classified as "property". That said its still not good to take losses. Making 10k is always better than losing 10k even if it lets you defer taxes on other earnings.

62303887
>>62303351
It has to be the same type of capital loss and gains though. You can't offset short term loss against long term gains.

62303938
>>62302190
correct, and you carry over anything not used to the next year

62304466
>>62303503
Why should we learn about a tax that we will never get to pay?

62304653
>>62303887
Look how confident this utter retard is. Lot of retards in here though excluding a couple anons.
It initially applies directly to the same type of capital gain/loss (short/long term). I’ll just give an example cause it’s harder to type out and explain it simply.

let’s say you
>realize a loss of 15k in a short term trade in Feb of 2026
>in April, you close another short term trade and realize a gain of $2500 in April.
>That puts you at -12500 on the year so far. Short for short, straightforward and as expected.
>You make no more sales of short term holdings for the rest of the year.
>in November, you sell a long term position and profit 9500 ltcg.
->since you have no more short term gains to cancel out, you can apply it to your long term gain
>you would end up net -3k on the year which you can then deduct from you MAGI
And ideally if you’re really scheming and planning, you obviously don’t want to realize a long term capital gain to cut down on a realized short term loss. It’s possible in incorrect about that last sentence. I won’t be smug like the brainlet I replied to. From my understanding you can’t just defer that long term realized gain

Here’s somethin I was thinking about as a possible plan I was contemplating (although I am aware about it carrying forward and I could use my “loss Bank” later in the future) , I have 19k in realized losses short from being a Stink marine already this year. I have long term precious metals, which are taxed at a rate of 28% on long term holds. I could realize my profits and sell some early gold bars that I got for low 2k as well as silver at 30, and use those short term losses to completely cancel out my realized profits in PMs 1:1.

62304666
>>62301796
>henlo IRS I losted $37,000 and want a refund
>yes I can hold

item.title: What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire? — 42 replies — 93.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire?

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-06-02 16:44 UTC

OP

What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire? Assuming one lives in USA

Early replies (first 3)

62299698
>>62299690
Just do nothing and wait, we will all be billionaires within the next decade

62299702
>Assuming one lives in USA
the first step is to get the fuck out of that dangerous brown shithole and go to Europe

62299711
>>62299690
Inheritance

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62299981
>>62299936
top signal

62299985
>>62299690
become a trophy husbando and marry some gay homosexual faggot dude or worse, marry some rich arab fugly hairy bitch

62300041
>>62299942
hasn't VGT outperformed though for like the past decade? doesn't it more sense to concentrate on tech?
>>62299936
is there any reason why the bubble wouldn't just pop this year? can you reason why it's not just a greater fool asset?
>>62299985
I'd be too miserable

62300109
>>62300041
The tech sector outperforms, but its downturn will also be much harder compared to a broader, more defensive index like VOO (hence "lower stress")

62300120
>>62299698
kek

62300255
>>62299690
$1x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2x2
Basically you can start with $1 and keep finding assets that just double (2x)
Is it likely you choose the correct asset 20 times in a row? Maybe not, or if you do it will involve a lot of reading and educating yourself to make correct choices + waiting for that 2x to happen.
But realistically you can start with more than just $1 in one single asset. $10 put into multiple assets that are below $1 (or really any price since the 2x is the real prize).
Speaking of realism, it's unlikely you ONLY pick assets that 2x, never choosing something that does a 3x, 5x, 10x, etc. I've spotted assets that did 30x in a year, dips that then did 10,000x.
There's also plenty of stuff I don't see because I'm not looking around with every hour of free time I have. I never started as low as $1, I almost always did $100 to $1000.
It really is easier than it seems because it's just one LONG process of educating yourself to make good choices. College/uni/trades/certifications are also A LOT of money + long hours of education in the hopes of making good choices on the tests. Then you hope there are job openings in your chosen field by time you're done, or you can move somewhere that has openings, or people will accept you for another field since you're proven you are a capable learner.
The thing is most people just never try, then many of the ones who do get complacent/greedy and lose hundreds of thousands. You could say endurance is the real "difficult" part, but don't get impatient. Be diligent and enjoy watching your earnings multiply over time.
Expect it to take a while, but since you don't control how high an asset goes when it pops-off it really might not take as long as you think. Better to be diligent about looking for opportunities and researching an asset's potential and if something does a crazy 100x, cool. If not it's still chill since that wasn't your expectation anyway.

62300263
>>62299690
Invest 333k into the s&p and wait 20 years

62300277
>>62299716
True, I'm up 50k with no effort from doing this.

62300285
>>62299702
oh another brown shithole. no thanks

62300337
>>62300041
You're miserable now, husbandmaxx your way into billions and financial security. You can divorce rape your troon husband afterwards

62300358
>>62299690
For me it's starting a business and hiring other people to do all the work

That would probably stress you out though if you've never done it

62300429
>>62299795
50/50 of nasdaq and snp, with a 5-10% of vxus or similar. More weighting towards the largest companies is better in the long run

62300484
>>62300041
>is there any reason why the bubble wouldn't just pop this year? can you reason why it's not just a greater fool asset?
is there any reason art industry would pop this year? can you reason why thats not just a great fool asset?

62300487
>>62300484
art/luxury brands/watches etc

62300731
>>62300484
>>62300487
Idk, I don't buy those. I figure it's just money laundering.

62300903
>>62300109
I guess, but it just seems like a waste if you're holding long term anyway

62300936
>>62299690
I guess buying gold, and putting it in a bank vault. Putting say a thousand dollar each month. Do this for 20 years.

62301038
>>62300903
In a bull market, you’re absolutely right. But if a bubble pops, you’re in for long years of suffering with tech stocks.

Reminder: if you bought a NASDAQ-100 index fund at the top of the dotcom bubble in 2000, you had to wait until 2015 (!!!) to be in the green again, even with so many great companies in the index. Meanwhile the S&P 500 recovery delay was less than 7 years.

Tech is not stress-free at all when the market goes south.

62301246
>>62299690
Selling pictures of your butthole on onlyfans

62301404
>>62300731
so? just buy and make money

62301415
Actuary-maxx (boring, kind of hard but not impossible)
Live below your means and save/invest as much as you want
No more than two children

62301431
>>62299690
The most common is to inherit $2 million from grandpa.

62302214
>>62299690
Haha at the retard in this thread shilling pokemon investing. Of all the brownoid traps I have ever seen, this is right at the top.

62302274
>>62299702
I'd rather not get stabbed by browns

62302488
>>62299690
Inherit a billion dollars and blow 99% of it partying.

62302523
>>62299690
Save about $2800 a month and invest it in something safe, like an index fund. At an average return of 7%, you'll have 2mil in 20 years.

Though, inflation will eat a lot of that up, at least 40%, and your 2mil will spend like 1.2mil. Invest about $4k a month instead to get ahead of it. Its the only way surefire way.

62302537
>>62302523
most people in the US don't make $4k per month after taxes, let alone have that much left over after expenses.

62304411
>>62302523
>invest 1 million a month in index funds, after only one month you’ll have 1 million (enough to retire)

62304496
>>62302537
Get a $96k per year remote job, and slum it in the deep south for 20 years. You might be able to get away living off only $48k a year. Or find the money some other way, I'm just telling you whats required.
>>62304411
You're forgetting about the magic of compound interest friend. $4k a month is only $960k after 20 years, coumpound interet is giving you a free million dollars.

62304513
buy link and delude yourself into believing $1k eoy. there are lots of (essentially) millionaires like this on this board

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 86 replies — 91.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 86 • Updated: 2026-06-02 16:14 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62289954
>>62287275
this. I just go all in bottom of the bear market and do nothing a couple years

62291704
>>62284814
This is no proper backtesting! You must test at least 30 instances!
https://youtu.be/zXoZdnYfIlA?si=MPB49se3bA1H2fnp

62291711
>>62291704
*32 Instances

62291719
>>62291711
>>62291704
?

62291779
>>62291719
How big are the wins and how big are the losses? They have to be in a profitable proportion together with the win rate. 66% win rate with wins 1/3 as big as the losses is not profitable for example.

62291951
>>62284814
broken clock o algo

62292012
what time of day do you make the trades

62292563
if you have a profitable strategy then just make a hyperliquid vault so we can join it and you have 10% profit share

62292730
>>62291779
There's a whole website and twitter account where you can check these things
>>62292012
>a broken clock predicts the future 80% of the time
That's one hell of a broken clock
>>62292012
The demo strategy executes ~2 hours into NY open, when there's enough FX data to confirm or inval the signal
>>62292563
Down the list of things to do. Right now it's a trade bot, which is being built because a mid-tier CEX wants to explore white labelling signals, so the bot needs to be optimized, but then I'll absolutely integrate it into HL

62292891
>>62292730
Thank you, I already have working strategies. I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.

62292918
>>62292891
>I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.
Always a good practice - prediction/outcome goes back to December 2024 which is where the hit rate is from. I could backtest to mid-2024 (when the FX layer was added) if I feel like it but I figured 100+ signals over more than a year was good enough

62293541
Spoonfeed me pls OP? Am I supposed to follow your twitter? How do I get the meme lines for myself.

62293559
>>62293541
>How do I get the meme lines for myself.
I'll post them in here, but there's also a live dashboard and a twitter, where a follow/likes would be greatly appreciated
>>62285878

62293571
>>62293559
Thanks for doing the needful sir. I'll be checking this out. Big ups anon.

62293619
>>62292730
Does the trading bot have contingencies in place just in case other trading bots start countertrading your strategy if it ends up being too successful?

62293680
>>62293619
I think if someone else's trading bot can move BTC by a few percent per day then we all have much bigger problems

62294595
>>62284814
Just buy the dip and sell the high simple. The main thing is to hold. I'm not only holding, I also staked some of my BTC recently since we can do that now without bridge involved.

62296711
BEHOLD, THE FUTURE

62296738
>>62296711

62297476
AAAAAAAAAAAAAA

62298184
>>62296711
Is that you Michael?

62298251
>>62284814
>buy signal
>5/28
This aged like milk.

62299491
>>62298251
>can't read
Maybe investing is not for you anon

>>62298184
Shhh it's a secret

62301262
>>62292563
>>62292730
what's a hyperliquid vault

62301440
>tfw if I listened to the schizo chart I would be down 10%

62301542
All i can see is that you've been incorrect? Am i missing something? Lol

62302104
>>62301542
>>62301440
We'll see - the hit rate's 78%, not 100%. The general sense, per the twitter thread yesterday, is bounce to the 5th-7th, then drop to the real bottom mid-June and then pump through summer, but obviously Brownian motion can't predict Saylor selling lol

62303698
Tell me no one bought because of this indicator please HAHAHAHAHA

62303805
Is this some intraday trading indicator? Like you were supposed to buy and then sell with leverage the day of the OP? If you would have bought and were holding now you would be fucked

62304332
reverse indicator of op is printing me money

item.title: who are these types of homes built for? >$390k to share a wall with a stranger >HOA fee exists but isn't disclosed on li… — 49 replies — 71.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
who are these types of homes built for? >$390k to share a wall with a stranger >HOA fee exists but isn't disclosed on li…

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-06-02 16:30 UTC

OP

who are these types of homes built for?
>$390k to share a wall with a stranger
>HOA fee exists but isn't disclosed on listing
>no trees, no privacy shrubs, living there will put you on SSRIs
>undoubtedly built by drunken illiterate retard spics in 3 weeks
>costs 20% more than a SFH existing build (with trees, a lawn, NO SHARED WALLS, etc.)
you'd need to be the world's biggest goy retard to consider these.

Early replies (first 3)

62298165
for people dealing in the real world

other questions?

62298175
>>62298161
>no trees, no privacy shrubs,
If only the buyer could add those himself later, choosing whatever he prefers.

Oh well, sadly that's impossible.

62298195
>>62298161
everything about these is absolutely dreadful. it feels like you're living in an apartment (except you're on the hook for $390k over 30 years + HOA + property taxes + tip.)
>GRAY, GRAY, GRAY
>layout designed by someone with downs syndrome + FAS
yeah uh i want a single basin sink island that's barely large enough to fit a cutting board and i want it in the middle of my OPEN CONCEPT gaynigger living room + kitchen (where is the dining room? who the fuck knows) with a view of my barren wasteland front yard.

WAIT -- I'M NOT DONE -- give me a bunch of ugly ass light fixtures and drywall features to cover the oversized beam that supports the open concept ceiling. and i DO NOT want crown molding on my ugly ass gray walls because that would add too much to the cost.

oh, and can you make the stairwell barely wide enough to fit an adult? i want it to be FUCKING IMPOSSIBLE to move furniture or appliances in or out of my GOYBOX.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298404
>>62298384
>not having to do 2 hours of work a week 3 months out of the year is worth living in a barren wasteland
no thanks, I’ll keep my trees

62298408
>>62298384
GOY spotted. raking leaves is a mentally ill suburban boomer phenomena. i'm puzzled by the scenario in your goybrain where trees are apparently only to be planted in areas that hang over your car? also why is your car not parked in the garage? (i know the answer. it's because you're a mentally ill suburban boomer. your garage isn't for parking vehicles, it's packed floor to ceiling with 1500 square feet of worthless nicknacks and other retarded garbage.)

62298416
The people that buy these are plebs who know nothing about basic construction/being handy, and associate anything "new" as being superior. True, there is no lead or asbestos, but the place will be falling apart within 20 years, and it is inhuman to pay that much to live in such close proximity. Homes from the 60s-70s have much better quality materials and larger lot sizes.

62298431
>>62298384
>>62298408
>OH YEAH, A RAKE? AND A LAWNMOWER? WHERE AM I GONNA KEEP THAT? I GOT NO ROOM IN MY GARAGE.
>LISTEN -- LISTEN, I KNOW IT'S CALLED A ONE "CAR" GARAGE BUT.... THAT'S JUST AN EXPRESSION!!!! A FIGURE OF SPEECH!!!!!

62298446
>>62298384
>>62298408
>>62298431
>don't you -- don't you FUCKING touch that motorcycle tire!!!!!
>it is NOT garbage!!!!!

62298546
>>62298195
>>62298207
>>62298273
>>62298310

OOOOOOO He's priced out!

Don't feel bad rentoid, I own multiple properties and also would not buy that Overpriced HOA shit.

There's a point that the younger generation has to realize what the boomers did. DIY while being totally unqualified to do it, ignore all the faggy permitting and regulations, build your own if you own the land.

62298565
>>62298546
who said i'm priced out? i make $150k and i'm looking at homes that cost $350-400k. i work from home so i have a lot of flexibility.

62298583
>>62298161
This thing looks either a made up fake computer graphic or ikea the house

62298588
read OPs posts with Nick Rochefort's voice

62298787
>>62298565

If true, wtf are you so close to Niggerdelphia?

Need easy access to ur trans hookers?

Real estate has always been about the location dawg.

62298816
>>62298310
>>62298161
These are european style shitboxes why the fuck are you having them?

62298828
>>62298787
niggerdelphia is an hour away. i might be LAID off one day, so i want to stay close to jobs that pay more than $16/hour.

62299240
Buying a house that has been made within the last 15 years is retarded. Guaranteed to have many problems.

62299332
>>62298161
Peak goybox.

62299341
>>62298175
If it is indeed part of a HOA, it is impossible

62299373
>>62298583
It says TO BE BUILT you fucking retard, yes it's been created on a computer

62300682
>>62298249
>it's either ceiling fans or heat pump
The... what? Ever heard of AC?

62300745
It's all optimized to make the most profit for the developer if you dont build your own house or buy something old.

62300830
>>62298161
They make them with shared walls because it results in a contiguous garden, instead of two small slices of yard with a house in the middle. It makes the small plot seem as if it has an okayish yard.
It's also slightly more energy efficient, so you save $3.50 on the montly energy bill

62300911
>>62298249
Why would you want a ceiling fan unc fr fr no cap

62300928
>>62300830
the question should be is it built with sound proofing in mind? if built cheaply answer of course no but you absolutely can design a house with that in mind so even feral shooting niglets couldn't be heard

62303461
>>62298161
this looks like the lowest possible quality of house. any lower and it would be just a bigger version of one of those Fischer-Price plastic play houses for toddlers

62303524
>>62300928
Hello no they’re not and you can rest assured that any conversation or sex noises can be heard by either side standing within ten feet of the party wall.

These builds are great option of you enjoy having zero privacy at all, with an added bonus of being constantly audio recorded if your new neighbor decides to install interior security cameras.

62303835
>>62303524
based. i'd make asmr videos of the sounds as it seems that kind of shit is guaranteed thousands to even millions of views

62303879
>>62298431
Everyone in my neighbourhood has a garage that looks just like this. It's fucking disgusting

62303883
>>62298161
>>62298273

>>$390k to share a wall with a stranger
These house prices are currently collapsing.
Wait.

62303903
>>62303461
>this looks like the lowest possible quality of house.
>any lower and it would be just a bigger version of one of those Fischer-Price plastic play houses for toddlers
Yep. Built by mexican's too, they are all crooked and fall apart .
Mexicans aren't geniuses home builders , you can tell if you visit mexico .
Maximum profits for italian construction companies .
That's why they want open borders btw, so that they can get cheap mexican labor.

62303913
>>62298161
Why bother, you can't afford it anyway

62304311
>>62298161
The honest answer to your question is that it is built for an investor who will rent it out to some basic chad bros for $2650/mo, pocket $300 month plus never return the deposit. Meanwhile, the Chad Bros pay his mortgage and the house increasin value 6.7% a year compounding and due to tax laws, all the income is tax free and then the investor, after 15 years, moves into it for 2 years and then sells it for $750k tax free.

~fin~

62304436
>>62298249
They put bare minimum and you are supposed to replace that shitty cheap light with a fan yourself

item.title: I TOLD YOU SO MUAHAHAHA >>62288629 — 26 replies — 46.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I TOLD YOU SO MUAHAHAHA >>62288629

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-02 16:37 UTC

OP

I TOLD YOU SO MUAHAHAHA

>>62288629

https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/crypto/articles/michael-saylors-bitcoin-treasury-firm-122216992.html

Early replies (first 3)

62298232
Bloody bastard! He made promise he wouldnt sell god fucking damnit

62298234
Anyone gets the feeling that Saylor won’t be part of the next bull run?

62298235
It's not enough, Saylor needs to be totally cleaned out for crypto to ever rise again.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298248
I hope this retarded permabull learns from this lesson

62298253
buy high, sell low

the biz way

62298267
BTC sub 60k today

Chainlink suppression killed it

62298280
>>62298216
Kek what a retard

62298598
32 btc sold, literal nothing burger.

62298622
>>62298598
32 today, it's only the beginning, once we are sub 60k he will panic sell thousands. Screencap this.

62298715
Pretty bearish for the asset backed ponzi scheme it's having to liquidate asset instead of just diluting more.

62298807
This was announced one month ago during the earnings call:

"We'll probably sell some Bitcoin to fund a dividend just to inoculate the market—just to send the message that we did it,” he said during the Q&A.

100% of the posters in this thread so far didn't acknowledge this, which proves his point that the headline will cause a lot of anti-BTC celebrations, so it is better to get the headline out of the way when the time is right.

Company officials already stated many months ago that they might sell BTC if it is considered helpful. For example, if the company's common stock is severely undervalued, trading some of its BTC for stock is probably a good deal.

The idea that the company cannot ever possibly sell any BTC was always absurd (Saylor's early statements on this probably didn't help), and they're right to start breaking this imagined taboo because people (like the posters here) only read the headlines.

62301366
>>62298807
>Saylor's early statements on this probably didn't help
Correct. Too many this is the only thing that matters.

62301536
>>62298232
he's selling to fund STRC dividends retard. this is what keeps the flywheel going

62302510
>>62302507
if only you knew how bad things really are

62302513
>>62301536
strc scam is collapsing, he barely has enough to pay dividends for a couple months

62302528
>>62298807
they are so broke that they needed 32 BTC?

62302544
>>62302507
Did he really post this? If so, that's an interesting message.

62302756
He sold a little bit now so in the future when he sells again he can say, look, it’s no big deal, I’ve sold before remember?

62302777
>>62302507
>last name is saylor
>he's getting off the boat
holy pottery

62302806
>>62298216
LUNA 2.0 inc

the moron is paying 11.5% on money he dont got

62302821
>>62302777
checked

IT'S OVER

62302859
>>62302544
Yeah. I interpreted it as Saylor is fleeing Bitcoin the sinking ship on his personal life raft if his investor’s money

62304470
either this shit is about to crater, or he only did it because there was money to be made on polymarket betting he did. either way, the real bull run doesnt begin until saylor gets liquidated

62304478
Can someone tell me where he is getting money to service his debt?

item.title: crypto shills destroyed /biz/ — 55 replies — 32.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
crypto shills destroyed /biz/

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-06-02 15:29 UTC

OP

you stupid shitcunts nobody is going to buy your fucken dumb digital coin that has no fucken use case

You were too late for bitcoin

you got psychologically destroyed by millennial and gen x goobers posting on twitter from their rented condos in miami

And now you're here trying to tiktok dance your way into fucken millions

GTFO

Early replies (first 3)

62293753
>>62293749
don't you have some dying boomer business to take care of lol

62293756
>>62293753
wowee

*does the fortnite dance*

feed my more low i.q. shortform slop sir!

Imagine being a GEN Z male who cannot even focus long enough to fill out a fucken business plan

62293763
>>62293756
imagine being so small brained that you have to work with dirty pipes since its the only way you wont lose everything. its indeed better you leave the crypto to smarter people and focus on the piss and shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294219
>>62294208
>>pokemon cards
i think you're confusing anon here, i never mentioned anything about trading cards.

62294221
>>62294219
My mistake. You are still a homosexual zoomer for investing in shitcoins.

62294231
>>62294221
wow, that was a very rude thing to say.

62294248
>>62293749
>crypto shills destroyed /biz/
/biz/ was created specifically to contain crypto shilling

62294250
>>62294194
>Chainlink solves multiple real world problems.
stopped reading right there, baggie

62294305
>>62294194
>Here's a really simple example for you: tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. This will enable the whole world to have exposure to hard money
Explain what this means. Seriously, explain it. Tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. How is that any different than buying 'gold' on an exchange that's just a certificate? They're both digital, but one of them is just stored on a rube-goldberg mechanism instead of a simple database. How does it enable "the whole world" to have exposure to hard money?

You're just saying fucking words.

62294322
>>62294305
>how are tokenized assets different from legacy assets??

t.

62294326
>>62294322
Yes. In a qualitative way, not a quantitative way, explain how they are fundamentally different. Being stored on a slightly different digital substrate doesn't change : you hold a -promise- that you own gold, not actual physical gold.

62294328
>>62294326
Ask your local AI fuckhead.
Nobody here wants to explain the absolute basics of tokenization to you, even the banks have been trying to explain the benefits for over half a decade.

62294331
>>62294328
I want to see you explain it, because you can't, without regurgitating another explanation that will be fille with just as much of a smokescreen.

62294337
>>62294331
listen to yourself

62294340
>>62294337
>a really simple example for you
You're the one who said it. If it's so simple, surely you can bang out a coherent explanation in a minute or two.

You're a tech fetishist. You are mentally bound to this retarded world because you imagine it makes you smart, but it's really just smart in the "grandma thinks he's good with computer" kind of way.

62294365
>>62294340
Take a deep breath you fat boomer, your bp is spiking.

62294367
>>62293749
These sluts must know they are creating wankmaterial. They arent fooling anyone. Skanks. I hate it when they trick my brain into wanting to put my penis inside them. They would be raped 24/7 in the old days.

62294368
>>62294365
I accept your concession.

62294378
>>62293749
That's not even her real body. The app program she's using starts stuttering to keep up as she turns to the side. She's probably built like a soup can.

62294385
>>62293749
use cases are bearish desu

62294446
>>62294385
Probably why memes did so well last cycle. Why i am buying blue chip memes again while they are cooked.

62294649
>>62293756
can you clean my pipe?

62294739
>>62293749
I was at a Calgary Flames hockey game last year and there was a boomer ahead of me who must have drank 7 or 8 tallboys and was just watching shit like this on his phablet.

Sometimes you gotta respect the absolute zero regard boomers have.

62296803
>>62294194
nobody wants hard money lmfao
soft money is how the system works because they can just run w/e fucken scams they want
the system isn't for the people brother
gold was done away with for this exact reason

62298686
>>62293845
if you're so smart, why aren't you rich?
turns out you're fucking stupid.

62299806
>>62293749
just dont be too late for xrp

62301355
>>62293749
anyone who after years has not taken their involvement in crypto or more broadly business beyond shitposting about tokens on a uruguayan cattle milking forum is ngmi

62301664
I'm still bagholding shitcoins that I bought 4 years ago and I'm worse off now than when I first got into crypto. I feel nothing. No hope, no emotions. I don't know wtf to do anymore in life. I hardly have the energy to go to the cage. I just want my life to end

62301988
>>62301664
Take the loss, move the money into actual investments. An index fund, whatever. Watch it grow, because it is based on physical reality (the output of the entire country). Keep contributing to it. 10 years from now it will have compounded so much you'll forget this whole thing.

Literally, just be a normie. Normies are fucking rich these days as long as they invest carefully and don't just pour everything down doordash and rent.

62302350
>>62301988
>Yes goy, sell the crypto bottom and buy the S&P top

62302354
>>62293749
XMR has more of a use case than BTC at this point

62302369
>>62302350
>Yep. The S&P top! It's over boys. The stock market is never going up again. It's the end of all things! Human productive activity has ceased! (please buy my shitcoin which will somehow have meaning in this apocalyptic state)

62304020
>>62301988
>it is based on physical reality (the output of the entire country)
so it's the top then

62303993
>>62303823

62304057

62304069

62304185

just holding 1 btc in cold storage now (which i will never sell)

now i can leave and never pay attention to this board again

62304200
>>62304185

62304209
>>62304185

62304217

item.title: The europoor paradox — 8 replies — 21.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The europoor paradox

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-02 16:49 UTC

OP

Why do we call them Yuropoors and laugh at their shitty $40k annual salaries, yet a random ass house on one of their Mediterranean islands is $3.2 million euros? How is their real estate so expensive if their wages are trash?

Like I make 400k a year in murricuh and I have no hope of being able to buy a very average property in Europe unless my NW hits $10 mil maybe

Early replies (first 3)

62303450
>>62303431
Retard, you are looking at huge houses in Mallorca, the equivelant would be me looking at 10000 square feet mansions in Marthas wineyard and presuming all burgers live like that.

>t.yuropoor living in a 160square meter house with 22ha land in Lapland

62303468
>>62303431
Those who own these houses are the ones who *pay* your shitty salary

62303491
People into their 40s are living with their parents.
Then inheriting something if they're lucky.
That's it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62303661
>>62303450
Not really.

>>62303431
K shaped economy, obviously.

62303663
>>62303431
bizarre bait

62303738
>>62303661
Here in Lux, that’s the price for a random 2-bedroom apartment in a building that looks like this

62303765
>>62303738
I can see the graffiti in 20 years when it's become social housing.
I mean, AI will enforce citizens to be on their best behavior, but the two tier society will continue, obviously.

62304540
>>62303431
because the houses are not bought by us yuropoors. They are bought by chinese and russians who want to get money out of their country

item.title: Bottom is $25,252.40. BTC drops 80% every cycle. — 17 replies — 19.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bottom is $25,252.40. BTC drops 80% every cycle.

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-02 16:18 UTC

OP

Bottom is $25,252.40. BTC drops 80% every cycle.

Early replies (first 3)

62303795
>Bottom is $25,252.40
I just hope whatever the bottom is, Gaylor has to sell everything. Bitcoin is meant for the people, not the juice. Saylor needs to leave and the Wall Street fags need to stay out.

62303809
>>62303795
But it's made to converge to a dozen owners, "for the people" would be a miracle against mathematical certainty.

62303830
>>62303809
How so? Holding bitcoin doesn't produce anything. You need to put in labor to buy more bitcoin. It's the opposite of the fiat capital market where new money first enters the hands of the haves before trickling to the have-nots.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62303877
>>62303766
25k btc would be fantastic to SLURP
but I doubt that

62303899
>>62303795
>Bottom is $25,252.40. BTC drops 80% every cycle.

very true !!

>>62303795
Gaylor belongs in a jail

62303908
>>62303830
>It's the opposite of the fiat capital market where new money first enters the hands of the haves before trickling to the have-nots.
Yes, that's what I said.

62303948
So I should open a short?

62304009
>>62303948

62304074
>>62303948
Yes, stop loss at 74k, enjoy the ride fren

62304106
>>62303766
>80% every cycle
Reducing each time
94%, 86%, 84%, 77%, [TBD]

Most likely floor is 35k for this cycle.

62304141
>>62303766
Yeah bro, buy the magical bottom of a dead asset that is going to $0.

>>62304074
Extremely Based. You're going to make life-changing money.

62304167
>>62304106
Because the market in general (including stocks) is gets more bullish over the years. Now imagine a stock bear market happening in the middle of a crypto bear market.

62304169
>>62304106
I'm slurping at $38500

62304170
>>62303766
Cryptofags are going to realize their asset that has no top also has no bottom

Enjoy your digital beanie babies

62304210
>>62304167
Stock market bear? We are heading to an unprecedent bubble burst + death spiral from Gaylor which is a 10x Luna size

62304226
>>62304210
Yeah, we’re talking about a big big bear here. I’m 100% cash and sleeping peacefully desu.

62304357
>>62304210
Saylor can sustain BTC dropping to $25k and staying there for many months. This notion that he is going to be 'liquidated' is pure bear cope.

Institutions are vacuuming up BTC faster than it's being mined.

item.title: What major asset will the top performer next decade? — 12 replies — 16.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
What major asset will the top performer next decade?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-02 16:26 UTC

OP

What major asset will the top performer next decade? BTC? NVDA?

Early replies (first 3)

62304212
>>62304204
DigiByte

62304215
>>62304204
Hyundai or Xiaomi will be the meme stonks

62304220
>>62304204
Chinese battery and solar, maybe robotics

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62304223
>>62304204
Food

62304227
>>62304204
The sex worker industry.

62304232
>>62304212
1B market cap min

>>62304215
hmm why's that?

>>62304220
which to invest in particular?

62304335
Human breeding in the incubator

62304365
>>62304232
They are both into physical AI, Xiaomi is the Apple of Chyna, Hyundai is known for decent products.
Would you buy a home or trades robot from Tesla or from Hyundai?

62304373
>>62304232
CATL will eventually become the largest company on earth. And they have good margins.
>Solar
shit margins, cutthroat competition.
>maybe robotics
hear me: the only people who will make big bucks in future robotics will be the ones that solve the issue of system integration in a proprietary platform (it is the most expensive part of automation) and the ones that may sell very specific components for humanoid robots (including, perhaps, the semiconductos, special actuators, maybe).
Robots themselves, humanoid or not, will commodified.

62304398
>>62304373
That's why I listed these two. Normally, you'd buy these integrated bots from Germans, but as they pivoted to promote managers by body height exclusively, they are now a decade behind incel Asia.

62304406
>>62304204
cbrs will have the marketcap of nvda+mu

62304412
>>62304406
Did you already buy in? I usually wait a month or two after IPO

item.title: I don't think BTC is how Satoshi described Bitcoin to be in 2008/9. — 4 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I don't think BTC is how Satoshi described Bitcoin to be in 2008/9.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-02 16:41 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62302848
>>62302844
At its core it's still what it always was, magic thinking

62303301
>>62302844
It's much improved now.
Block size is finally restricted, to avoid making some shitshow of a chain that is hard to verify fully, and that puts remote miners at disadvantage due to lag.

But you will not get any of this.

62304484
>>62303301
>But you will not get any of this
i just hope you wont get any of those poor kids, i hope they escape

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62304498
Nick Szabo speaks on behalf of Hal Finney and he's fine with the current state of Bitcoin, so fuck off

item.title: No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this. — 45 replies — 116.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this.

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-06-02 16:48 UTC

OP

Saylor sells 32 BTC out of 843,000+ BTC and anon starts acting like Bitcoin is dead.

You're not a real BTC holder.

Early replies (first 3)

62299202
why did he sell 32 btc tho?

62299205
>>62299202
to get a higher rating from some corporate wagie on wall street. he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.

62299325
Didn't he tell people to never sell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62300746
>>62299202
For some reason.

62300755
>>62299205
He can do whatever he wants tho

62300807
>>62299325
Yeah before but I don't mind

62300810
>ponzi scheme can't dilute on the back of a speculative asset anymore
Buttcoin is over.

62300991
>>62299187
This is how it starts dumbass, why sell BTC at all if he's got money? especially selling at a loss says alot.

62301317
>>62300807
I don't either, I have no skin in the game. Just funny how the poster child for btc is going back on his word.

62301382
The fact that crypto is even a thing shows how most people have zero understanding of money or value.

62301632
>>62301590
Absolute bullshit as usual.

Microsoft, Coca-Cola or Boeing don’t sell their intellectual property; it doesn’t mean this IP has no value, that it’s ignored by rating agencies, or that it’s not real.

The only person who suggested MSTR would never sell its BTC is Saylor himself; no one ever told him his BTC "can’t be sold" or prevented him from selling anything. He cornered himself into this position by his own volition.

And selling 32 BTC only proves that you had 32 BTC, nothing more. The only unquestionable method to prove your Bitcoin is "real" would be to give us the adresses of your wallets, Mike. The entire world could check the state of your BTC reserves in real time. But you never want to reveal this basic info. Why?

Lies lies and lies.

62301643
>>62301590
beta

62302272
>>62301317
I don't care who the major players are that sell their BTC. It doesn't really affect me since I'm focused on the long term. That's what I mean.

62302278
>>62299379
but every BTC holder can sell at will. That's not exactly unique.

62302621
>>62299533
He has a choice.

62302691
>>62299533
>buy 20k btc
>price drops .5%
>sell 32 btc
>price drops 5%
He just can’t win

62302729
>>62299202
The company is extremely opportunistic. At times it accumulated bitcoin using cash, at times it used common stock dilution, at times it used corporate bonds, and now it is using preferred stock. A dogma like "we will never sell ANY" bitcoin is unreasonable because:
- It isn't how the real world works. Anything on any company's balance sheet is subject to selling if the circumstances demand it. It is part of its creditworthiness, valuation, and so on
- It severely limits opportunities

Many months ago Strategy already acknowledged that it might sell bitcoin tactically (while staying long strategically) to:
- Pay back debt
- Pay dividends
- Buy back common stock if it becomes too undervalued

They stated the reason for the current sale during their last earnings call a month ago: the goal is to get the headline "Saylor is selling" out at a controlled point in time and price in the irrational panic from breaking the apparent taboo. Check out any forum, 4chan, reddit, twitter, comments are nearly 100% about "this ship is sinking, Saylor is broke, ponzi collapsing", because they only read the headline that Saylor is selling without bothering to look into why he's doing it.

62302766
>>62302691
He can if he sold earlier

62302807
>>62299187
thats like saying "oh satoshi only sold 32 btc"

of course its a big deal if this cucksaylor with $10billion of debt and 800,000 btc sells some

62302809
>>62302807
Saylor cuckold fanboys are too retarded to understand this. Imagine worshipping a boomer faggot from the dotcom bubble who spent 6 years buying "dips" and is now down -5 billion dollars kek

62302811
>>62302809
the sentiment is obvious outside of delusional saylormaxis, maybe not soon but eventually this house of cards topples

62302815
>>62302811
the scary part is how plenty of BTC maxis fell for his bullshit. MSTR is a giant ponzi

62303046
>SELL ORGANS KEEP BITCOIN NEVER SELL BITCOIN
>sells insanely small fraction of bitcoin
>see it's literally nothing, a drop in the ocean
>also he's just selling to prove he can for the bankers because such a small fraction provides confidence it's all truly worth that
retarded

62303090
>>62302809
>full stops instead of commas
you third worlders ruined this board

62303101
>>62299379
Not an mstr holder but getting their debt rated is probably going to open up the capital markets to them.

62303404
>>62299564
Is my 12btc in danger? I keep holding

62303647
>>62299187
he's just refinancing, sell 400k btc at 65k, rebuy at 20k, sell bitcoin to buy bitcoin, diamond hands

62303655
>>62299202
to buy more bitcoin at cheaper price, when yoi're holding so much btc market manipulation = refinancting

62303658
>>62299187
Have you ever heard of a death spiral? The lower it goes, the more he needs to sell.

Kek - baggies.

62303726
>>62299202
it was a test send. i always send a small amount first to see if i got the right wallet, etc.

62304527
>>62303404
>>62302272
If you held through March 2020, Dec 2022, Feb 2026 then I'm sure you will continue to hold
BUT you are literally missing out on the biggest bullrun in stock market history
You are literally sidelined right now and its clear people are rotating out of BTC and into stock (AI boom) even if you think its a bubble

62304534
>>62303090
>t. shitskin amerimutt
we use it like this in Europe

item.title: I have 46 BTC. They used to be the dominant asset in my portfolio, but now I feel like they're a time bomb. Should I sel… — 19 replies — 19.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have 46 BTC. They used to be the dominant asset in my portfolio, but now I feel like they're a time bomb. Should I sel…

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-02 16:12 UTC

OP

I have 46 BTC. They used to be the dominant asset in my portfolio, but now I feel like they're a time bomb. Should I sell?

Early replies (first 3)

62296412
>>62296205
Send address so i can check on Blockchain

62296423
>>62296205

Buy Polkadot near all-time low desu

62296432
>>62296205

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298496
>>62296205
>Should I sell?
you can invest into my project if you would like.

1) platform like Steam, but you forever own your game (not a revokable licence), and much more perks/social for gamers. you can resell used game (but keeping it = more perks). On blockchain. (not a shitcoin, just mechanism to note ownership).

2) a game about hacking but actually realistic

sounds interesting?

0) level: get in on the ground floor (tm) - frens, anons, family, (((you))) possibly
1) level: small incubators (100-300$ k USD), small reserach grants if any. (using part of profit to pay up level 0 investors)
2) level: bigger investors, possibly IPO
3) focusing on profits from the end users.

62298534
>>62296205
Sell 20btc for the US30Y. That would balance it out perfectly.

62298594
>>62298496
We already discussed this in another thread, but the problem of your project is that you’re competing against billion-dollar companies with decades of existence, but you have almost no capital, no experience (?), no moat (blockchain ownership to resell games is not something *both* players and devs really desire), no existing customers and no network effect (the good developers won’t be selling here in the beginning, so it won’t take off).

Check how Garena did to become a major platform with a solid moat right away.

62298668
>>62296205
>1oz .999 Copper Coin
Thats as real as bitcoin will ever be, by the way, 1oz of copper is currently €0.36, Thats what a real bitcoin is actually worth.

62298833
>>62298668

62301495
>>62298594
>We already discussed this in another thread, but the problem of your project is that
>you’re competing against billion-dollar companies with decades of existence,
that's ok for me

>but you have almost no capital,
can do the programming work myself. marketing comes later, that's mainly for the first investors and next level of investor (but I believe in word of mouth as well)
>no experience (?)
programming/blockchain

>same no moat (blockchain ownership to resell games is not something *both* players and devs really desire),
>no existing customers and no network effect (the good developers won’t be selling here in the beginning, so it won’t take off).
it's for gamers who want real ownership of games, and game developers (indie) who are ok with providing that instead of predatory practices like EA and many others
not every game studio will want that, but that's fine

I was thinking of the problem - of game devels being "scared" of their game being re-sold.
1) this "problem" is not that big. yard sales were always possible in good old times of game CDs, and never caused some large issue for game developers. piracy alone caused far more
2) first person - buying it "brand new" will get extra perks;
3) you lose many perks when you sell the game - first of: you can no longer [legally] play it; you also don't receive updates. leaderboards, achievements, badges

other perks, for curren-owners include community (more open to current-owners) and various other "social" aspects. they do matter do some part of gamers.
